adjustment_rationale: Reference-case adjustment kept neutral to preserve the baseline scenario as the calibration anchor.
Baseline treatment row remains unperturbed so scenario contrasts are interpreted relative to the observed regional mix.
Reference-case adjustment kept neutral to avoid embedding undocumented technology preference into the baseline portfolio.
Small positive energy calibration retained for baseline pyrolysis to reflect manuscript scenario differentiation while preserving traceability.
High-supply case modestly improves AD scale economics while preserving higher residual carbon accounting than policy-supported conditions.
Baseline treatment row remains unperturbed so supply-side sensitivity acts on candidate technologies rather than the reference waste-treatment mix.
High-supply case improves HTC energy and cost performance to create an auditable alternative to pyrolysis under relaxed feed availability.
High-supply case softens pyrolysis competitiveness to avoid hard-coded dominance and to expose constraint sensitivity in scenario runs.
Policy-support case emphasizes AD environmental and cost gains to reflect supportive incentive assumptions in a reviewer-traceable table.
Baseline treatment row remains unperturbed so policy support applies to candidate pathways and not to the external counterfactual.
Policy support moderately favors HTC through cost and carbon-load multipliers while keeping the calibration explicit and reviewable.
Policy support provides only a mild pyrolysis uplift so scenario divergence remains attributable to traceable policy assumptions rather than hidden constants.
